description: Create a specification document (docs/spec-port-init-scripts.md) in the repo that details the plan to port the shell-based init sequence into Go. This serves as the reference spec for future work. Also update the README to mention the init subsystem.
steps: Create docs/ directory if it does not exist

Write docs/spec-port-init-scripts.md containing: overview of the current shell-based init flow (from overlay/init through bootstrap, mode detection, mode-specific scripts, boot, cleanup, exec /sbin/init), the target Go architecture (new internal/init/ package with sub-packages for each phase), the mapping of shell functions to Go functions, interface definitions for testability, TDD/SOLID/KISS/DRY principles applied, and phased implementation approach
Update README.md to briefly mention the init subsystem and link to the spec
Run golangci-lint (no Go changes but good practice to check)
Commit with message 'docs: add specification for porting init scripts to Go'
acceptance_criteria: docs/spec-port-init-scripts.md exists and contains comprehensive specification
findings: Spec written covering all 10 shell scripts. golangci-lint passes clean. README updated with init subsystem section linking to spec.
